candidates.why: Direct top-line revenue label; preferred over duplicate total row.
Duplicate total revenue row with identical values; lower priority than the more direct revenue label.

variables.notes: Used priority list for revenue label changes; later code can take first non-missing value.
No clear cost of goods sold / cost of sales line is present in the Income Statement preview.

candidates.why: Clear operating overhead component and explicitly listed candidate; excludes depreciation and finance items.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A total row is present. Selected the clearest overhead component from candidates and excluded total rows, depreciation, and finance items.

candidates.why: Most direct R&D-style label on the income statement.
Supplemental development expense line that may be populated in some periods.

variables.notes: Used priority list for possible R&D label variation.

candidates.why: Direct interest expense line; preferred for XINT.
Supplemental interest expense line if primary line is missing in some periods.

variables.notes: Used priority list for interest expense. Did not use Financial net or Non-operating Income/(Expense), Net because a direct interest line exists.

candidates.why: Best available equity line; preferable to combined equity/liabilities totals.
Combined/awkward total that appears duplicative of Total equity; lower quality.
Full balance sheet total, not book equity.

variables.notes: No parent/owners-only equity line is present, so used Total equity as BE. Per rule, MIB is set to [] to avoid double counting.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation line, but MIB should preferably come from balance sheet.
Income statement allocation line, but MIB should preferably come from balance sheet.
Direct balance sheet NCI label, but left unused because BE uses Total equity and including MIB would risk double counting.
Direct balance sheet minority interest label, but left unused because BE uses Total equity and including MIB would risk double counting.

variables.notes: Set to [] because BE uses Total equity, which would generally already include any non-controlling interests; this avoids double counting in BE + MIB.
